findings.title: Scan job tools are registered but missing from layer map
findings.evidence: TOOL_DEFINITIONS registers memory_index_scan_status and memory_index_scan_cancel at .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts:943-945, but the L7 tools list at .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/architecture/layer-definitions.ts:115 includes memory_index_scan and related maintenance tools without the two new scan job tools. The guard at .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tests/layer-definitions.vitest.ts:156-162 expects every registered tool to have a layer definition.
findings.recommendation: Add memory_index_scan_status and memory_index_scan_cancel to the L7 Maintenance tools list so TOOL_LAYER_MAP and getLayerForTool stay aligned with TOOL_DEFINITIONS.

findings.title: Stale tool count guard still expects 37
findings.evidence: .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tests/review-fixes.vitest.ts:117 asserts TOOL_DEFINITIONS.length is 37, while the current canonical parity test asserts 39 at .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tests/spec-memory-cli-parity-and-help.vitest.ts:126 and the committed ownership fixture includes memory_index_scan_status/cancel at .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tests/fixtures/tool-ownership-map.json:145-158.
findings.recommendation: Update this count guard to 39 or derive it from the canonical expected tool list to avoid a stale hard-coded count.
